title: tinycrypt SHA-256 return values not checked
description:
tinycrypt functions return TC_CRYPTO_SUCCESS (1) on success and TC_CRYPTO_FAIL (0) on failure. The return values of tc_sha256_init(), tc_sha256_update(), and tc_sha256_final() are not checked.

Locations:
- ipv6_addr.c:60-62 (lichen_pubkey_to_iid)
- lora_l2.c:90-93 (generate_eui64)

What goes wrong: If tinycrypt encounters an internal error (e.g., NULL internal state), the hash output is undefined. The code would then use garbage bytes as the IID or EUI-64, potentially causing:
- Duplicate addresses across nodes (collision)
- Non-deterministic addresses across reboots
- Silent cryptographic failure

Fix: Check return values and return -EIO or fall back appropriately on failure.
